zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan le Frauncis於1312年出生在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾特朗平頓時，她的父親Alan De Frauncis22歲，母親Lady Joan Foremark25歲。她於1327年與John de Bassingbourne在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾坎布利其結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1371年在英國坎布利其爾多丁頓享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan le Frauncis于1312年出生在英国英格兰坎布利其爾特朗平頓时，她的父亲Alan De Frauncis22岁，母亲Lady Joan Foremark25岁。她于1327年与John de Bassingbourne在英国英格兰坎布利其爾坎布利其结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1371年在英国坎布利其爾多丁頓享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Frauncis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Old French personal name Franceis, modern French François, from Latin Franciscus, originally ‘Frank’, though later used to denote a Frenchman. The personal name owed much of its popularity during the Middle Ages to the fame of Saint Francis of Assisi (1181–1226), whose baptismal name was actually Giovanni but who was nicknamed Francisco because his father was absent in France at the time of his birth. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Francesco and Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian, and Croatian Franc , and also their derivatives, e.g. Croatian Francišković (see Francisco 4).
English: ethnic name from the Old French adjective Franceis ‘French’ (see 1 above).
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames, or an adoption of the non-Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ferenc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Casimir, Iwan, Zbigniew.
Hungarian, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia and Poland), Slovenian, and Croatian: from the personal name Ferenc, a Hungarian form of Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Fraczek
meanings: Polish (Frączek): from a pet form of the personal name Franciszek, an equivalent of Latin Franciscus (see Francis ). It is found mainly in Lesser Poland.

variants.name: Chico
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jesus, Pedro, Rafael, Bernardo, Camilo, Cirilo, Juan, Miguel, Ramona, Ruben.
Spanish: from chico ‘small, young’ (of uncertain origin, perhaps from Latin ciccum ‘trifle’), applied as a nickname for a small man, or for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name.
Portuguese: from a pet form of the personal name Francisco, a vernacular form of Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Francesco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Gabriele, Giovanni, Pasquale, Rocco, Salvatore.  Spanish Carlota, Garcia, Romana.
Italian: from the personal name Francesco (from Latin Franciscus), an equivalent of Francis .

variants.name: Di Cicco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Pasquale, Carlo, Cosmo, Giovanni, Giulio, Giuseppe, Nicola, Attilio, Benedetto, Carmela.
Italian: patronymic from a pet form of the personal name Francesco, from Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Ceccarelli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Dino, Angelo, Dante, Quinto, Sesto, Aldo, Americo, Amerigo, Arcangelo, Augusto, Aurelio, Franco, Gino, Guido, Italo, Leoncio, Renato, Roberto, Tino, Tito, Valerio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Ceccarello, a diminutive of Cecco, itself a diminutive Francesco, from Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Di Cioccio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Cosmo, Mario, Cesidio, Dante, Guido, Salvatore, Aldo, Americo, Benito, Biagio, Domenico, Ezio, Flavio, Nunzio, Quintino, Silverio.
Italian: patronymic from a personal name of uncertain derivation,possibly a pet form of Francesco (see Francis ).

variants.name: Cillo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Americo, Aniello, Antonio, Carmine, Gianna, Giovanni, Guido, Livio, Rocco, Vito.
Italian: from a shortened pet form of Francesco (equivalent of Francis ).

variants.name: Cisco
meanings: Italian (Veneto): from the personal name Cisco, a short form of Francesco, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).
American shortened form of the surname   Francisco   (which is in part of German and presumably also of French origin) and thus a variant of   Sisco   and   Sesco  .

variants.name: Fran
meanings: Croatian: from a short form of local equivalents of the Latin personal name Franciscus (see Francis ).
Spanish: probably an altered form of Catalan   Franc  .
Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from a short form of the Spanish personal name   Franco   or   Francisco  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan le Frauncis was born in 1312, in Trumpington, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Alan De Frauncis, was 22 and her mother, Lady Joan Foremark, was 25. She married John de Bassingbourne in 1327, in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died in 1371, in Doddington, Cambridgeshire, England, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan le Frauncis est née en 1312, à Trumpington, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Alan De Frauncis, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Lady Joan Foremark, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé John de Bassingbourne en 1327, à Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1371, à Doddington, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan le Frauncis 1312 in Trumpington, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alan De Frauncis 22 und ihre Mutter Lady Joan Foremark 25. Sie hat 1327 in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom John de Bassingbourne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1371 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Doddington, Cambridgeshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan le Frauncis è nata il nel 1312 nel Trumpington, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Alan De Frauncis, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Lady Joan Foremark, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato John de Bassingbourne nel 1327, nel Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1371, nel Doddington, Cambridgeshire, England, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan le Frauncisが1312年にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアトルンパントンで生まれた時，父Alan De Frauncisは22歳，母Lady Joan Foremarkは25歳であった。1327年にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアカンブリッジでJohn de Bassingbourneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1371年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアドッドイントンで59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan le Frauncis(이)가 1312년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 트럼핀그턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alan De Frauncis(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Joan Foremark(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1327년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 캄브리지에서 John de Bassingbourne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1371년 영국 캄브리지실르 도딩턴에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan le Frauncis nasceu em 1312, em Trumpington, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Alan De Frauncis, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Lady Joan Foremark, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com John de Bassingbourne em 1327, em Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1371, em Doddington, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan le Frauncis родилась в 1312 г. в местности трумпингтон, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Alan De Frauncis было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Joan Foremark, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Bassingbourne в 1327 г. в местности камбридж, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1371 г. в местности доддингтон, камбриджешир, Англия в возрасте 59 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan le Frauncis nació en 1312, en Trumpington, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Alan De Frauncis, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Lady Joan Foremark, tenía 25 años. Se casó con John de Bassingbourne en 1327, en Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió en 1371, en Doddington, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 59 años.
